It was able to run so much faster, it could look **twice as many steps ahead** than the opposing Agent.

## Publications

### Gymnasium: A Standard Interface for Reinforcement Learning Environments

Published by: Mark Towers, Ariel Kwiatkowski, Rodrigo P., Jun Jet T.      // 杰, Omar Younis

July 24, 2024

Gymnasium is an open-source library providing an API for reinforcement learning environments. Its main contribution is a central abstraction for wide interoperability between benchmark environments and training algorithms. Gymnasium comes with various built-in environments and utilities to simplify researchers’ work along with being supported by most training libraries. This paper outlines the main design decisions for Gymnasium, its key features, and the differences to alternative APIs. Show less

View Publication

### A comprehensive analysis of agent factorization and learning  algorithms in multiagent systems

Published by: Stavros Orfanoudakis, Georgios Chalkiadakis

June 16, 2024

In multiagent systems, agent factorization denotes the process of segmenting the state-action space of the environment into distinct components, each corresponding to an individual agent, and subsequently determining the interactions among these agents. Effective agent factorization significantly influences the system performance of real-world industrial applications. In this work, we try to assess the performance impact of agent factorization when using different learning algorithms in multiagent coordination settings; and thus discover the source of performance quality of the multiagent solution derived by combining different factorizations with different learning algorithms. To this end, we evaluate twelve different agent factorization instances—or agent definitions—in the warehouse traffic management domain, comparing the training performance of three learning algorithms suitable for learning coordinated multiagent policies: the ES, the CES, and CCEA. Our results demonstrate that the performance of different learning algorithms is affected in different ways by alternative agent definitions. Given this, we can conclude that many important multiagent coordination problems can eventually be solved more efficiently by a suitable agent factorization combined with an appropriate choice of a learning algorithm. Moreover, our work shows that ES and CES are effective learning algorithms for the warehouse traffic management domain, while, interestingly, celebrated policy gradient methods do not fare well in this complex real-world problem setting. More broadly, our work demonstrates the need to identify appropriate agent definitions-multiagent learning algorithm pairings in order to solve specific complex problems effectively, and provides insights into the general characteristics that such pairings must possess to address broad classes of multiagent learning and coordination problems. Show less

View Publication

### The Performance Impact of Combining Agent Factorization with Different Learning Algorithms for Multiagent Coordination

Published by: Stavros Orfanoudakis, Georgios Chalkiadakis

September 9, 2022

Video Presentention: https://odysee.com/@Kallinteris-Andreas:7/The-Performance-Impact-of-Combining-Agent-Factorization-with-Different-Learning-Algorithms-for-Multiagent-Coordination:8 ABSTRACT: Factorizing a multiagent system refers to partitioning the state-action space to individual agents and defining the interactions between those agents. This so-called agent factorization is of much importance in real-world industrial settings, and is a process that can have significant performance implications. In this work, we explore if the performance impact of agent factorization is different when using different learning algorithms in multiagent coordination settings. We evaluated six different agent factorization instances—or agent definitions—in the warehouse traffic management domain, comparing the performance of (mainly) two learning algorithms suitable for learning coordinated multiagent policies: the Evolutionary Strategies (ES), and a genetic algorithm (CCEA) previously used in this setting. Our results demonstrate that different learning algorithms are affected in different ways by alternative agent definitions. Given this, we can deduce that many important multiagent coordination problems can potentially be solved by an appropriate agent factorization in conjunction with an appropriate choice of a learning algorithm. Moreover, our work shows that ES is an effective learning algorithm for the warehouse traffic management domain; while, interestingly, celebrated policy gradient methods do not fare well in this complex real-world problem setting. Show less

View Publication

# Evgenii Dushkin

Senior Machine Learning Researcher @ Munster Technological University | Deep RL | Unitree Robotics | NVIDIA Isaac Sim | NVIDIA Isaac Lab

Senior Machine Learning Researcher at Munster Technological University

Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland (IE)

894 connections • 1,093 followers

## About

With over a decade of professional experience in software development in robotics field focusing on Reinforcement Learning approaches and 3D vision systems through cutting-edge Machine Learning technologies. We leverage expertise in Deep Reinforcement Learning, employing frameworks like PyTorch to tackle challenges in automation and motion control. Collaborating with diverse teams, we integrate state-of-the-art machine learning approaches like APPO to deliver solutions for complex environments including MuJoCo, Unity, and Gymnasium. Our mission is to innovate scalable AI-driven applications in robotics while driving impactful outcomes for research and development in industrial automation and beyond.

Total Experience: 12 years and 2 months

## Experience

### Senior Machine Learning Researcher at Munster Technological University (Current)

Nov 2022 - Present • 3 years and 1 month

Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 2021 • Educational • Higher Education

At the IMaR Research Centre (Munster Technological University) I lead applied research in robotics and 3D vision. Built and maintained an RL stack in NVIDIA Isaac-Sim (Isaac Lab) for the Unitree G1 humanoid: custom manager-based environments, command generators (target vectors, binary masks), observation pipelines, contact sensors, curriculum and domain randomization. Trained with PPO/APPO (PyTorch, rsl_rl) and tracked with TensorBoard. Designed locomotion objectives: masked joint-target tracking; penalties for foot crossing, too-narrow/too-wide stance vs pelvis width; foot alignment to commanded heading; base stabilization (orientation/velocities). Implemented teleop/telemetry: UDP bridge for base velocity, joint targets and masks into Isaac Lab command terms; DOF-limit normalization; safe resets; logging and video export. Prepared models for sim2real: accurate joint limits and actuator models, inertia/friction tuning, sensor-noise filtering, torque clamping—improving stability and reproducibility. MuJoCo experience: classic & locomotion benchmarks (CartPole, Acrobot, Hopper, Walker2d, Humanoid); algorithms PPO, APPO, A2C, REINFORCE, DQN; imitation learning (Behavior Cloning, DAgger); reward shaping and observation/reward normalization. Unity ML-Agents: curriculum for 2-vs-2 humanoid soccer, MARL coordination, episode annotation and automated logging. Built LLM systems: custom Transformer architectures (Seq2Seq, Encoder-Decoder, Decoder-Only GPT) with distributed multi-GPU training in PyTorch. Robotics & 3D vision: fully automated calibration for a robot-mounted 3D camera on KUKA (TensorFlow; improved SGD) — reduced calibration time 2h → 2min and improved accuracy ×10 vs AX=XB on the same datasets; presented at scientific venues (audiences 100+). Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZBKmIPm2C-E Applied RL beyond robotics: automated trading with APPO (PyTorch) achieving a stable ~25% personal-account gain. Show less

Department: Research • Level: Senior

## Projects

### Nvidia Isaac Lab — execbat Contributor Build (Unitree G1 23-DOF Ballet Teleop)

Jul 2025 - Present

Contributor build by execbat (Evgenii Dushkin) on top of NVIDIA Isaac Lab / Isaac Sim (PyTorch, GPU-accelerated) with two custom environments for the Unitree G1 (23-DoF). The project trains a humanoid to dance ballet and to mirror external motions: a real robot repeats poses from the virtual scene while keeping balance and following live velocity commands. How it works: Tech stack: Isaac Lab 4.5 + Isaac Sim, PyTorch, RSL-RL, CUDA/RTX; teleop over UDP. Envs: training env for RL; play/teleop env that ingests a 49-float packet (23 joint targets, 23 DoF mask, 3 base velocities). Control: command terms for base_velocity, target_joint_pose, dof_mask; manager-based pipeline. Learning: adaptive curriculum + custom rewards (foot flatness/alignment, no cross-over, stride width). Uniqueness: seamless sim-to-real mirroring with live joint-masking; the robot can follow choreography from sim while reacting to user commands. Show less

### RL approach for Classic Control task in MuJoCo environment

May 2025 - May 2025

MuJoCo Inverted Pendulum Target. This project provides a custom MuJoCo environment where an agent controls an inverted pendulum. The task is to move the pole tip to a randomly spawned target and hold balance near the target point. Mass of the pendulum is variable. The environment is designed for training with APPO GPU(Asynchronous Proximal Policy Optimization) algorithm. Also available for PPO CPU / PPO GPU. Repo: https://github.com/execbat/mujoco_inverted_pendulum_project

### Deep Reinforcement Learning approach for Stock Market trading

Jan 2022 - Mar 2022

This repository provides the scenario of how to solve the problem of automated trading. Stock market environment built on engine of OpenAI's Gymnasium environment which emulates behavior of real stock market environment. Integration with Sample Factory implemented for efficient training of reinforcement learning (RL) policy based on asynchronous proximal policy optimization (APPO). Trained RL policy takes actions to increase balance of personal account.

Added on-line control of all events. ♦ Performing a DryRun test cycle of automatic stations ♦ Execution of the work cycle of the automatic station with welding of the car body Knowledge and using of the industrial standards: •KUKA MTR standard •Fanuc RARN standard Show less

Within this project, I have successfully implemented laser soldering for various parts like the roof, sidewall, and trunk, using Electro Galvanized Steel as the part material. Additionally, I conducted research to adapt the laser soldering technology from Electro Galvanized (EG) to Hot-Dip Galvanized (HDG) main material, specifically for butt and overlap joint types. results.text:
# Evgenii Dushkin

Senior Machine Learning Researcher @ Munster Technological University | Deep RL | Unitree Robotics | NVIDIA Isaac Sim | NVIDIA Isaac Lab

Senior Machine Learning Researcher at Munster Technological University

Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland (IE)

893 connections • 1,091 followers

## About

With over a decade of professional experience in software development in robotics field focusing on Reinforcement Learning approaches and 3D vision systems through cutting-edge Machine Learning technologies. We leverage expertise in Deep Reinforcement Learning, employing frameworks like PyTorch to tackle challenges in automation and motion control. Collaborating with diverse teams, we integrate state-of-the-art machine learning approaches like APPO to deliver solutions for complex environments including MuJoCo, Unity, and Gymnasium. Our mission is to innovate scalable AI-driven applications in robotics while driving impactful outcomes for research and development in industrial automation and beyond.

Total Experience: 12 years and 2 months

## Experience

### Senior Machine Learning Researcher at Munster Technological University (Current)

Nov 2022 - Present • 3 years and 1 month

Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 2021 • Educational • Higher Education

At the IMaR Research Centre (Munster Technological University) I lead applied research in robotics and 3D vision. Built and maintained an RL stack in NVIDIA Isaac-Sim (Isaac Lab) for the Unitree G1 humanoid: custom manager-based environments, command generators (target vectors, binary masks), observation pipelines, contact sensors, curriculum and domain randomization. Trained with PPO/APPO (PyTorch, rsl_rl) and tracked with TensorBoard. Designed locomotion objectives: masked joint-target tracking; penalties for foot crossing, too-narrow/too-wide stance vs pelvis width; foot alignment to commanded heading; base stabilization (orientation/velocities). Implemented teleop/telemetry: UDP bridge for base velocity, joint targets and masks into Isaac Lab command terms; DOF-limit normalization; safe resets; logging and video export. Prepared models for sim2real: accurate joint limits and actuator models, inertia/friction tuning, sensor-noise filtering, torque clamping—improving stability and reproducibility. MuJoCo experience: classic & locomotion benchmarks (CartPole, Acrobot, Hopper, Walker2d, Humanoid); algorithms PPO, APPO, A2C, REINFORCE, DQN; imitation learning (Behavior Cloning, DAgger); reward shaping and observation/reward normalization. Unity ML-Agents: curriculum for 2-vs-2 humanoid soccer, MARL coordination, episode annotation and automated logging. Built LLM systems: custom Transformer architectures (Seq2Seq, Encoder-Decoder, Decoder-Only GPT) with distributed multi-GPU training in PyTorch. Robotics & 3D vision: fully automated calibration for a robot-mounted 3D camera on KUKA (TensorFlow; improved SGD) — reduced calibration time 2h → 2min and improved accuracy ×10 vs AX=XB on the same datasets; presented at scientific venues (audiences 100+). Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZBKmIPm2C-E Applied RL beyond robotics: automated trading with APPO (PyTorch) achieving a stable ~25% personal-account gain. Show less

Department: Research • Level: Senior

## Projects

### Nvidia Isaac Lab — execbat Contributor Build (Unitree G1 23-DOF Ballet Teleop)

Jul 2025 - Present

Contributor build by execbat (Evgenii Dushkin) on top of NVIDIA Isaac Lab / Isaac Sim (PyTorch, GPU-accelerated) with two custom environments for the Unitree G1 (23-DoF). The project trains a humanoid to dance ballet and to mirror external motions: a real robot repeats poses from the virtual scene while keeping balance and following live velocity commands. How it works: Tech stack: Isaac Lab 4.5 + Isaac Sim, PyTorch, RSL-RL, CUDA/RTX; teleop over UDP. Envs: training env for RL; play/teleop env that ingests a 49-float packet (23 joint targets, 23 DoF mask, 3 base velocities). Control: command terms for base_velocity, target_joint_pose, dof_mask; manager-based pipeline. Learning: adaptive curriculum + custom rewards (foot flatness/alignment, no cross-over, stride width). Uniqueness: seamless sim-to-real mirroring with live joint-masking; the robot can follow choreography from sim while reacting to user commands. Show less

### RL approach for Classic Control task in MuJoCo environment

May 2025 - May 2025

MuJoCo Inverted Pendulum Target. This project provides a custom MuJoCo environment where an agent controls an inverted pendulum. The task is to move the pole tip to a randomly spawned target and hold balance near the target point. Mass of the pendulum is variable. The environment is designed for training with APPO GPU(Asynchronous Proximal Policy Optimization) algorithm. Also available for PPO CPU / PPO GPU. Repo: https://github.com/execbat/mujoco_inverted_pendulum_project

### Deep Reinforcement Learning approach for Stock Market trading

Jan 2022 - Mar 2022

This repository provides the scenario of how to solve the problem of automated trading. Stock market environment built on engine of OpenAI's Gymnasium environment which emulates behavior of real stock market environment. Integration with Sample Factory implemented for efficient training of reinforcement learning (RL) policy based on asynchronous proximal policy optimization (APPO). Trained RL policy takes actions to increase balance of personal account.

Oct 2020 - Nov 2020

Mobile Laser Cladding complex: The best solution when you can't deliver the part to robotic station for cladding. So Robotic station can be moved to the site where this part is locate. Role: Independently implement of all stages of the complex - form Idea to final cladding of the part ♦ Made deep analisys of the requirements for the implementation of laser technology ♦ Defined the concept of a robotic complex, agreeing with the customer ♦ Prepared the list of equipment, assessment of its compatibility, coordination with the customer ♦ Adapted of the laser technology software from KSS8.3 to KSS8.6 KUKA KRC4 ♦ Developed of the HMI laser system KSS8.6 KUKA KRC4 ♦ Prepared and connected the Laser Fiber with Laser Head (laser source: IPG YLS4000 & Laser Head IPG D50) ♦ Made Adjustment, Calibration, Testing of Laser equipment ♦ Calculation of technological parameters of laser cladding. Preparation for surfacing a real product Used Hardware: - Laser source YLS-3000 - Laser head: IPG Photonics D50-Wobbling - KUKA KRC4 KSS8.6.5 KR70 R2100 IONTEC Used technologies: - Laser cladding with filler wire - Hybrid cladding (Laser + MIG\MAG) - Laser cladding with hot wire (optional) Show less

### Deep Laser Cladding station

Sep 2020 - Nov 2020

Full independently creation of Robotic Laser Station for cladding technology. Robotic station specified for Laser Cladding Technology at hard-to-reach surfaces (inside of pipes) ♦ Analysis of the customer's requirements and needs for the implementation of laser technology ♦ Independently extedned the kinematic scheme of the KUKA KRC4 robot ♦ Chose the components for the KUKA KRC4 modernization, coordination with the customer ♦ Adapted KUKA KRC2 linear unit to a KUKA KRC4 controller ♦ Performed electrical switching of new components inside the KRC4 controller independently ♦ Implemented of Automatical Self-Correction of working distance for Laser Head during the cladding process ♦ Developed of Robot Control Software and HMI for laser technology (for KUKA KSS8.3) ♦ Made the process of Preparing and Connecting the Laser Fiber to the Laser Head (IPG YLS20000 laser source & Nittany Laser DBCH_3500 laser head ) Used Hardware: - Laser source YLS-10 - Laser head: Nittany laser DBCH-2500 - KUKA KRC4 KR2700 R120 quantec extra HA - Linear unit Zollern - Two-axis positioner KUKA DKP-400 Used technologies: - Laser cladding with filler wire - Laser cladding with hot wire (optional) - Laser cladding with metal powder (optional) Show less

### SK381 Škoda Octavia A8

Jul 2019 - Jan 2020

The Škoda Octavia A8 project involved robotics automation at the bodyshop of Volkswagen-Skoda vehicle manufacturing. As an Expert of Laser Brazing technology, Laser Equipment, and KUKA robot programming, my role included programming 317 robots, with 49 new robots launched. I was responsible for setting up laser source parameters for the LaserLine LDF4.000-30 and configuring the Scansonic ALO3 laser head. Within this project, I have successfully implemented laser soldering for various parts like the roof, sidewall, and trunk, using Electro Galvanized Steel as the part material. Additionally, I conducted research to adapt the laser soldering technology from Electro Galvanized (EG) to Hot-Dip Galvanized (HDG) main material, specifically for butt and overlap joint types. I have shared my research findings and pinned them on my LinkedIn profile. Show less

### SK326/0 Škoda Karoq

Apr 2019 - Dec 2019

The Škoda Karoq project involved robotics automation at the bodyshop of Volkswagen-Skoda vehicle manufacturing. As an Expert of Laser Brazing technology, Laser Equipment, and KUKA robot programming, my role included programming 297 robots, with 49 new robots launched. I was responsible for setting up laser source parameters for the LaserLine LDF4.000-30 and configuring the Scansonic ALO3 laser head. Within this project, I have successfully implemented laser soldering for various parts like the roof, sidewall, and trunk, using Electro Galvanized Steel as the part material. Additionally, I conducted research to adapt the laser soldering technology from Electro Galvanized (EG) to Hot-Dip Galvanized (HDG) main material, specifically for butt and overlap joint types. I have shared my research findings and pinned them on my LinkedIn profile. Show less

### MVM2

Jul 2018 - Feb 2019

MVM2 - Robotic station for welding parts of subway carriages Role: Robotics programmer / Safety system programmer ♦ Modernization of laser station "MVM1" ♦ Added the flexibility of the working cycle. The user could chose the full cycle OR only a separate stage of working cycle. ♦ Improved fault tolerance. Changed safety program of SICK safety controller ♦ Improved user-friendliness. The structure of the program has been completely redesigned. The complex is equipped with: • Siemens 1200 plc • KUKA KR C4 2700 Quantec x2 • Two draw-out tables on a linear unit. • Ytterbium laser IPG Photonics LS-6 x2. • Laser head IPG photonics 2D High power scanner x2. Show less

### REMAN

May 2018 - Jul 2018

REMAN: Station for Laser Cladding Technology using 3 Laser Heads The project involved the development of a Station for Laser Cladding Technology using 3 Laser Heads, in collaboration with IPG Photonics. Here are the key highlights of my involvement in the project: • Independently developed software: Successfully developed software for controlling laser technologies based on the KUKA KRC4 controller. This software allows for seamless integration and precise control of the laser processes. • Algorithm development: Devised and described algorithms for controlling various laser processes, including powder laser cladding, wire laser cladding, and thermal laser hardening. These algorithms enable precise and efficient laser-based material deposition. • Convenient HMI development: Created a user-friendly Human-Machine Interface (HMI) for writing and adjusting laser programs. This HMI simplifies the programming and adjustment processes, enhancing user productivity. • Library creation: Developed a library specifically designed for surfacing parts with spiral and ring trajectories. This library streamlines the surfacing process and ensures consistent and accurate results. • Automated tool change process: Successfully implemented a fully automated tool change process, allowing for quick and seamless switching between laser heads, minimizing downtime. • Enhanced mechanical security: Improved the mechanical security of the complex by implementing online control of all processes. This ensures safe and reliable operation of the system. Show less

Project Link

### KONAR

Sep 2017 - May 2018

KONAR: Station for Laser Cladding Technology using 3 Laser Heads The first implementation of Conception meaning 3 Laser Heads at 1 Robot The project involved the development of a Station for Laser Cladding Technology using 3 Laser Heads, in collaboration with IPG Photonics. Here are the key highlights of my involvement in the project: • Independently developed software: Successfully developed software for controlling laser technologies based on the KUKA KRC4 controller. This software allows for seamless integration and precise control of the laser processes. • Algorithm development: Devised and described algorithms for controlling various laser processes, including powder laser cladding, wire laser cladding, and thermal laser hardening. These algorithms enable precise and efficient laser-based material deposition. • Convenient HMI development: Created a user-friendly Human-Machine Interface (HMI) for writing and adjusting laser programs. This HMI simplifies the programming and adjustment processes, enhancing user productivity. • Library creation: Developed a library specifically designed for surfacing parts with spiral and ring trajectories. This library streamlines the surfacing process and ensures consistent and accurate results. • Automated tool change process: Successfully implemented a fully automated tool change process, allowing for quick and seamless switching between laser heads, minimizing downtime. • Enhanced mechanical security: Improved the mechanical security of the complex by implementing online control of all processes. This ensures safe and reliable operation of the system. Show less

## Recommendations

Graham Foran

“I’ve had the pleasure of working alongside Evgenii on the development of robotic systems in milking parlours. His depth of knowledge, particularly around KUKA robotics and fault finding, is second to none.

Evgenii doesn’t just deliver on the technical side his ongoing support after development has been crucial to its success. He’s solutions-focused, and always able to tackle challenges if they occur. If you’re working on a project and not quite sure how to bring it to life, Evgenii is exactly the kind of person you want on your team.

I would highly recommend him to anyone looking for reliable, expert-level support in robotics development and implementation.
”

Dmitry Bykovskiy

“I worked with Evgenii for several years.  We developed together robotic equipments based on fiber lasers.  I was responsible for welding technology, Eugenii - for automatization.  Eugenii took a responsible approach to creating projects for a robot and a controller.  Minor problems that arose during development were easily found by Evgenii and fixed quickly. This allowed us to reduce debugging time and focuse on development and reseaching.”

## About

## About

Hello ! I'm a french 2nd year PhD student at INRIA Bordeaux in the Flowers team. My…

Total Experience: 4 years and 8 months

## Experience

### PHD Student at Inria (Current)

Dec 2024 - Present • 1 year and 1 month

Bordeaux, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 1967 • Government Agency • Research Services

PhD in LLM for code generation in environment-agent interaction.

Department: Education • Level: Specialist

### Research Intern At Inria (FLOWERS Team) at Inria

May 2024 - Dec 2024 • 7 months

Bordeaux, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 1967 • Government Agency • Research Services

Research internship on the topic of "Evolving large populations of adaptive neural agents in ecologically plausible environments".

Department: Other • Level: Specialist

### Research Project With Google DeepMind at Google DeepMind

Oct 2023 - Apr 2024 • 6 months

Paris, Île-de-France, France

Company: 501-1000 employees • Founded 2010 • Privately Held • Research Services

Using population-based methods for improving convergence of state-of-the-art algorithms on 2-players 0-sum games. Research project supervised by Paul Muller, from the Google DeepMind’s Multiagent team. 📝 Code : github.com/tboulet/Algorithms-for-Normal-Form-Games

### Research And Development Intern at Ubisoft Montréal

Feb 2023 - Jul 2023 • 5 months

Montreal, Quebec, Canada

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 1997 • Public Company • Computer Games

A 6 months internship in Research & Development on the subject of Machine Learning applied to video games. - Reinforcement Learning : implementation of diverse algorithms and methods (BC, DQN, SAC, HER, ...) on the Ubisoft RL framework - Supervised Learning : use of basic ML methods and advanced neural network models for time series forecasting. The objective was to predict the next aim and position of a bot based on player data. ⚙️ Techs : pytorch, sklearn, C++, transformers

Department: Research • Level: Intern

### Research Intern at InstaDeep Ltd

Aug 2022 - Jan 2023 • 5 months

Paris, Île-de-France, France

Company: 201-500 employees • Founded 2014 • Privately Held • Software Development

A 6-months internship on research on various problems in the context of Marine Transportation. - Empty Container Repositioning problem : solving a complex realistic environment/MDP using multiagent RL and Operational Research methods - RL for Optimization : implementing Reinforcement Learning methods for the combinatorial optimization problems of Fleet Deployment and Network Design. 📝 Medium article written on the subject : https://medium.com/instadeep/deep-reinforcement-learning-for-network-design-in-marine-transportation-c3e31a1aba0 ⚙️Techs : ray's RLlib, pytorch, tensorflow, gym, WandB Show less

Department: Research • Level: Intern

### Creation And Teaching Of Courses On AI And Reinforcement Learning at Automatants - Association d'IA de CentraleSupélec

May 2022 - Nov 2022 • 6 months

Company: 11-50 employees • Founded 2018 • Nonprofit • Information Technology & Services

📝 GitHub repo : github.com/tboulet/Formation-Reinforcement-Learning ▶️ Course Video 1 (in French!) : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=juNSptzWTJs ▶️ Course Video 2 (in French!) : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LId8UpG_YY4 Creation and presentation all by myself of courses on artificial intelligence and more particularly on reinforcement learning, for the students of the University of Paris-Saclay campus. - Course on theorical Reinforcement Learning - Creation of a web playground for tabular RL deployed using Streamlit or Hugging Face's Gradio app - Implementation and teaching of Practical Works for students having to implement basic RL algorithms (Q-Learning, SARSA, MonteCarlo...) on a gridworld environnement. ⚙️Techs : streamlit, gradio, python, gym 🧪Theoretical notions : MDP Framework, Dynamic Programming, Bellman's Equations, TD Learning methods, Monte Carlo methods, Value-based vs. Policy-based methods, Deep Reinforcement Learning, parallelism, observation & reward shaping, Off Policy learning Show less

### RL Project Train A RL Agent To Play Mario at Automatants - Association d'IA de CentraleSupélec

Oct 2021 - Apr 2022 • 6 months

Company: 11-50 employees • Founded 2018 • Nonprofit • Information Technology & Services

Supervising a group of first years student for a RL project, as part of the Automatants projects - Implementation of different RL algorithms - Use and adapt these algorithm to perform on a Mario environment ⚙️ Techs : Pytorch, Gym, SB3, WandB

### Computer Vision Project Facial Expression Recognition at Automatants - Association d'IA de CentraleSupélec

Mar 2021 - Sep 2021 • 6 months

Company: 11-50 employees • Founded 2018 • Nonprofit • Information Technology & Services

Individual project to develop an application that can recognize facial expression - Implementation of the algorithm in tensorflow (preprocessing pipeline, model and prediction) - Research of datasets and training of the CNN model - Development of a small interactive game of emotion imitation ⚙️ Techs: Python, Tensorflow, Deep Learning, CNN, Open-CV

### Personal Projects And Initiatives During The Schooling At CentraleSupélec at CentraleSupélec

Sep 2020 - Nov 2022 • 2 years and 2 months

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 2015 • Educational • Higher Education

Computer Science projects: - Real Estate data analysis in connection with MeilleursAgents for predicting prices - Genetic algorithms - Optimization under constraints - Graph search problems : development of a python framework to create and solve graph problems (A star algorithm, ...) - Game theory : development of a python framework to create N-players stochastic turn-by-turn games. Implementation of AI agents for solving them (generalized MiniMax, MonteCarlo Tree Search, ...)

### Machine Learning Competition (1st 76) at CentraleSupélec

Jan 2022 - Feb 2022 • 1 month

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 2015 • Educational • Higher Education

3-weeks Kaggle competition for CentraleSupélec ML course : classification using tabular data obtained from satellite images representing different types of construction sites. - Feature extraction, selection and engineering, using many tools and python librairies (Geopandas, Shapely, Elliptic Fourier analysis) - Comparison between various ML models (SVM, kNN, Random Forest, XGBoost...) - 🏆 Winner : 1st place out of 76 teams ⚙️ Techs: Python, Pandas, Sklearn, XGBoost

### Medical Data Inference at CentraleSupélec

Apr 2021 - Apr 2021

Company: 1001-5000 employees • Founded 2015 • Educational • Higher Education

School project in connection with the Pasteur Institute. Use of medical data provided by the Pasteur Institute and development of data science models for obtaining medical information and statistics ⚙️ Techs: Python, R

### Student Project For Valeo Improved Semantic Segmentation For Night Drive at Valeo

Feb 2022 - Jun 2022 • 4 months

Company: 10,001+ employees • Founded 1923 • Public Company • Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing

Project aiming at improving semantic segmentation on driving images at night-time, using GAN and lightning correction methods. It was done in the frame of a semester project at CentraleSupélec, and was proposed by a team from Valeo, a french automotive supplier. GitHub repo is private for privacy reasons. - Segmentation models (U-net and DeepLab-v3) for driving - Use of Cycle-GANs (ForkGAN and CoMoGAN) for obtaining night-drive data from day-drive data - Lightning method on night-drive images to simulate car lights and increase performance ⚙️Techs : tensorflow 1, Deep Learning, gradio 🧪Theoretical notions : GANs, Cycle-GANs, segmentation, project management Show less

### Member at ViaRézo

Sep 2020 - Jul 2021 • 10 months

Company: 11-50 employees • Founded 2017 • Nonprofit • Computer Networking Products

ViaRézo is a student association in charge of providing an Internet access and many web services to more than 2000 students. ⚙️Techs : Git, Gitlab, SQL, python, Telegram bots

Department: Other • Level: Specialist

## Education

### Master MVA - Mathématiques, Vision, Apprentissage, 15.93/20, equivalent GPA : 3.7 at École normale supérieure Paris-Saclay

2023 - 2024 • 1 year

Gif-sur-Yvette, Île-de-France, FR

Courses : Algorithmes pour l’optimisation et la gestion des réseaux – Nancy Perrot Introduction to Probabilistic Graphical Models and Deep Generative Models – Pierre Latouche Deep Learning in Practice – Guillaume Charpiat Deep Learning – Vincent Lepetit Bayesian Machine Learning – Julyan Arbel Apprentissage et Génération par Échantillonnage de Probabilités – Stéphane Mallat Geometric Data Analysis – Jean Feydy

### Master of Science, Computer Science - Deep learning, GPA of 3.9 at CentraleSupélec

2020 - 2024 • 4 years

FR

Top 2 French Engineer School, CentraleSupélec (member of Paris Saclay University) is ranked 13th in the Shanghai global ranking. Joined after a highly selective selection process. 📚 First year relevant courses: • Mathematics (Convergence, Integration, Probability, Partial Differential Equation) • Statistics and Learning algorithms • Algorithmics and Complexity 📚 Second year relevant courses: • Optimization • Advanced statistics • Machine Learning • Artificial Intelligence 📚 Third year relevant courses: • Mathematics (Stochastic Integration, Harmonic Analysis, Modern Mathematical Morphology, Statistics) • Deep Learning in Practice (DLP) • Reinforcement Learning • Natural Language Processing • Machine Learning in Network Science (MLNS) Show less

### Bachelor's degree, Mathematics at Université Paris-Saclay

2020 - 2021 • 1 year

Mathematics degree obtained within a double cursus during the first year of the engineering school of CentraleSupélec.

### Classe préparatoire aux Grandes Écoles - Preparatory Program, Mathematics - Physics - Engineer Sciences, Admission to CentraleSupélec engineer school at Lycée Henri Loritz - Nancy

2018 - 2020 • 2 years

A two-years undergraduate intensive course in mathematics and physics leading up to competitive nationwide entrance examinations to engineer graduate schools.

## Skills

research • container • optimization • deployment • design • transportation • tensorflow • training • projects • analysis • algorithms • python • engineering • segmentation • driving • teaching • courses • youtube • intelligence • git • gitlab • sql • science • statistics • forecasting • problem solving • network design • computer vision • real estate • data analysis • genetic algorithms • machine learning • deep learning • artificial intelligence • web services • data science

## Projects

### Python packages

Sep 2020 - Present

I have developed various python packages and frameworks for AI related tasks: 🔥AI agents for Search Problems : a framework for Search Problems and Search Algorithms (DFS, A*,...) 📝 GitHub repo : https://github.com/tboulet/AI-Agents-for-Search-Problems 🔥AI agents for Games : a framework for Perfect Information Extensive-from Games (chess, connect4,...) and some algorithm ((Expecti)MiniMax, MCTS, ) 📝 GitHub repo : https://github.com/tboulet/AI-Agents-for-Games 🔥IntegOR : a framework based on scipy.optimize that aims to traduce an Operation Research problem that can be linearized to a binary integer linear problem, to solve it. 📝 GitHub repo : https://github.com/tboulet/IntegOR 🔥Backtracking : A framework for backtracking algorithm, as an interface and a solving function. 📝 GitHub repo : https://github.com/tboulet/backtracking_algorithm 🔥LocalPerf : an easy-to-use package for measuring the performance of python in any machine, in terms of CPU, multiprocessing and GPU (pytorch with CUDA), and also verify that the GPU is used. 📝 GitHub repo : https://github.com/tboulet/Local-Python-Performance Show less

### School Project : PCA-guided K-means

Dec 2023 - Dec 2023

A benchmark on various K-means initialization method, in particular PCA-guided search, as explained in the paper "PCA-guided search for K-means" by Qin Xu et al. This is a student project by Timothé Boulet, Thomas Lemercier and Théo Saulus. The goal was to reproduce the results of the paper, and also to benchmark various K-means initialization methods (random, KR, PCA-guided, etc...) and study the effect of overlapping and of the number of clusters on the performance.

### School Research Project : Spiking Neural Networks Benchmark

Sep 2023 - Sep 2023

A research project conducted at CentraleSupélec. The objective was to benchmark the SNN architecture on many tasks to see on which tasks the SNNs were the more performant regarding the trade-off accuracy/energy. Information on SNN : https://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2022/hash/c4e5f4de1b3cfc838eec6484d0b85378-Abstract-Conference.html

### Telegram bot : Sold-out Tickets Detector

Jul 2023 - Jul 2023

A bot to detect when tickets (festivals, concerts...) are sold out and send an alert. The bot will regularly check if any ticket in the watchlist is sold out. If so, it will send an alert to the associated telegram chat. The bot runs with python. It uses the Telegram API for the bot interface, Selenium for web scraping and SQlite for the database.

### Web App : RL Playground

May 2023 - May 2023

A playground for Reinforcement Learning algorithms. It is a simple app that allows you to play with different environments, algorithms, and hyperparameters.

### Repository : Stable Baselines 3 Pipeline

Apr 2023 - Apr 2023

A repo for training and enjoying trained (or not) SB3 agents on any environment, including custom ones. This is build on top of the Stable Baselines 3 library. It is highly configurable and provide wandb/tensorboard logging, loading and saving of agents, new policies such as the Permutation Invariant Neural Network.

### Deep RL Framework

Nov 2022 - Nov 2022

RLearn is a framework made by myself for Deep Reinforcement Learning made by myself. I implements diverse RL algorithms and try to make them learn on various environnements. It is based on the OpenAI Gym and pytorch. I was used in the framework of the AI student association of CentraleSupélec, Automatants, for discovering Deep RL.

### Gridworld Reinforcement Learning Environment and Framework

Oct 2022 - Oct 2022

This project implements a simple yet rich and variable gridworld environment for reinforcement learning, as well as tabular agents such as Q-learning and SARSA. This was made for students to discover basic tabular RL, and let them implement RL agents as part of practical works of the formation in Automatants.

### Student Project : Task Scheduling with A* and IDA*

Apr 2022 - Apr 2022

Implementation of an informed search algorithm A* and IDA* for solving a scheduling problem, aiming to optimize the use of several cores for completing interdependent tasks.

## Projects

### AutoRef AI

Jan 2026 - Present

-Problem: Unlike the big leagues, most high school / recretaional games do not have thousands of cameras for referees to replay and fairly judge and make calls. AutoRefAI will solve this via CV / pose reconstruction / segmetnation / etc for Fair Referring of games for recretional. - Aiming for a Demo V1 by Jan 30th. Furthermore, AutoRef AI will be 100% local first since many areas do not have good internet outside of the U.S, and millions of sports games are played everyday. -Gonna expand past basketball to soccer Show less

### CareerAgent 1.0

Jan 2026 - Present

-A State of the Art Career Agent with RAG, custom cover letters and resume applications, research agent to find applications, company culture (where I'd be a good fit, etc)

### Google Tunix Train a Model to Show Its reasoning Traces

Jan 2026 - Present

-Finished the full YouTube demo and Kaggle writeup and submitted -Finished RL GRPO on Chain of Thought Reasoning and Answer Tags, Achieving a training loss of near 0 and a training KL of train_kl=0.001 -RL achieved accuracy rates of 100% on <reasoning> </reasoning> <answer> </answer> and solid reasoning inside the reasoning tags -Developing a high quality notebook with a dmeo of Gemma 3 1b RL trained off of already SFTed notebook model -Finishing Writeup and YouTube video demo -Finished SFT, developing coherent reasoning and <reasoning> and </reasoning> and <answer> and </answer> tags -Finished the RL reward function and parameters with Tunix -Successfully debugged TPU distirbution problems via jax.sharding NamedSharding, jax.tree.map, and jax.device_put, allowing TPU 5ve-8 to add LoRA fine tuned weights and initalize the RL GRPO training. -Successfully started training RL GRPO (tested in Kaggle TPU 5ev-8 and running and saving, monitoring with Weights and Biases -almost finished RL, but realized the issue is context length > 1024 for Gemma 3 1b crashes RL on TPU and the unrolling graph compaliton of RL --> I figured out how to solve thie: create our own synthetic State of the Art Super Model (won't say which model until done) synthetic chain of thought for <reasoning> </reasoning> <answer></answer> that is <= 400 words (512 tokens) to prevent Gemma 3 1b it from crashing -Incorporating cool features for demo to add polish and coolness to it. -Can have posture detection if turned on (can work locally is goal) to help with bad posture + 20 20 20 rule for eye strain, play meditation music automatically, and also can intrepret your speech -Focusing all Effort on Tunix to January 12th (Polished demo / completion) Show less

### AI Investment Financial Oracle

Jan 2026 - Dec 2026

-Curretly training on AWS SageMaker a custom LSTM GRU with Mixture of Experts, Outputting Multiple Variance in Price Predictions -Fine Tuning Chronos Time Series Model on AWS SageMaker -Fine Tuning TimesFM Google Model on AWS SAgeMaker -Outside of SageMaker Studio training, S3 for initial weights / training, not using any services and prioritizing innovation to allow for maximum growth on investment over being chained to a cloud service once I finish this fire Tunix Demo -Utilizing an AI Agent with Langgraph/langchain and google agent sdk to help with predicting prices synthesizing the data -Planning a Demo Showing Price Prediction and Graph of Predictions vs. Actual for The Magnificent 7 (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA -Alpha Seekers: PLTR (Palantir), AMD, and AVGO (Broadcom) -Macro Weights: JPM (JPMorgan) and WMT (Walmart) -Aiming to achieve performance improvement (hopefully) over guestimate guesses -Aiming to Finish by January 2026, progress will speed up as soon as I finish with demo for Tunix by January 12th (RL still in training, ran into some WandB memory issues, disabled it, should be smooth sailing) Show less

### SOLAR PANEL DEFECT DETECTION - COMPARATIVE VISION MODEL RESEARCH

Nov 2025 - Dec 2025

- Benchmarked 6 state-of-the-art computer vision architectures (YOLOv12-XL, Qwen 3 VLM-8B, ConvNeXt V2, Swin Transformer V2, EfficientNet-B0, ResNet50) on binary classification task (crack vs. hotspot detection) - Designed and trained custom dual-attention CNN incorporating squeeze-excitation and coordinate attention mechanisms, achieving 100% test accuracy with 3x faster inference than transformer-based models - Fine-tuned vision-language model (Qwen 3 VLM-8B) using LoRA on 1,000+ thermal/UV solar panel images, achieving 75.6% accuracy -Achieved 100% Accuracy, Precision, F1, Recall Score fine tuning ResNet50 and Swin Transformer V2 -Fine tuned YOLOv12 extra large (59 million parameters) with ultralytics on 1,000 + thermal/UV solar panel images, achieving 95.73% accuracy, 95.73% Recall, 95.78% F1 - Demonstrated architectural understanding beats brute-force scaling through systematic model comparison - Deployed models to ONNX for faster inference and runtime and tested their runtime and accuracy on test set Tech stack: PyTorch, HuggingFace Transformers, Unsloth, trl SFT (Supervised Fine Tuning), Ultralytics YOLOv12, custom CNN architecture, ONNX optimization Show less

### Astro-Guard: Autonomous Multi-Agent Swarm for Aerospace QA | Kaggle Google AI Agents Capstone | Lead Architect & Mentor

Nov 2025 - Nov 2025

-Engineered an autonomous, hierarchical multi-agent system ("Astro-Guard") for high-stakes aerospace manufacturing QA, acting as an "always-on" reliability engineer to process sensor telemetry and address bottlenecks. -Mentored a teammate, Dimitry, on multi-agent system design, prompt engineering, and the use of the Google Gen AI SDK, leveraging the project as a leadership and teaching opportunity. Designed a Hybrid Sequential-Parallel Architecture using Google Gen AI SDK, deploying a Parallel Sensing Swarm of 3 specialized domain experts with gemini-2.5-flash-lite for parallel data analysis. Implemented a Sequential Reasoning Pipeline with a gemini-2.5-pro Meta-Analyzer Core to synthesize reports and autonomously search for component-specific mitigation strategies using a built-in Google Search tool. -Achieved critical safety and efficiency gains by detecting complex, compound failure modes (Thermal + Vibration harmonics) that single-variable thresholds miss. Optimized for MLOps cost by implementing a custom token-optimization tool to minify JSON sensor data before inference, significantly reducing API costs for high-throughput factory environments. Managed state with InMemorySessionService and a custom Logbook for persistent long-term critical failure tracking. Show less

### (Current Bronze Medal, Waiting for Private LB to Finish) NFL Big Data Bowl 2026 - Prediction

Oct 2025 - Nov 2025

-Achieved Bronze Medal with GRU + GNN + LSTM ensemble pipeline and spatial dynamic feature engineering -Rank: 111/1444 -Top 9.7%

### Pragmatic Orchestrator Aetherflow — Computational Linguistics Research Platform

Oct 2025 - Nov 2025

Creator & Lead Architect | 2025 -Built a local-first multi-agent computational pragmatics system that operationalizes linguistic theory (Searle 1969, Grice 1975, Myers-Scotton 1993, Montague 1973, Fillmore 1976) as executable code. -Engineered a felicity-condition routing engine computing illocutionary force vectors + Gricean maxim violations to dynamically select agents — supporting low-latency local inference. -Implemented 7 specialized linguistic agents (pragmatic router, multimodal analyzer, code-switching engine, semantic λ-calculus parser, frame-semantic memory, RLAIF critic, abductive explainer). -Designed multimodal pipeline combining Gemini 2.5 Flash + Google Cloud Vision OCR to interpret Chinese characters and compute visual implicatures from radical structure and stroke pressure. -Developed a typed semantic parser (Montague semantics) using Pydantic + λ-calculus constraints to prevent translation errors at runtime. -Built a lightweight episodic memory system (SQLite) encoding illocutionary force, implicature metadata, and RLHF ratings for adaptive learning. -Ran theoretical ablations: removing code-switch theory caused –23% F1 on bilingual tech discourse, empirically validating theory-driven routing. -Stack: Python, SQLite, Streamlit, Ollama (Qwen 3), AWS Bedrock (Claude), Google AI (Gemini 2.5 Flash), Google Cloud Vision, OpenCV, Pydantic. Show less

### OpenAI Red Teaming Hackathon (2025) – Alignment & Vulnerability Analysis

Aug 2025 - Sep 2025

-Developed novel red teaming framework targeting deceptive alignment and distributed multi-agent vulnerabilities in LLMs -Authored thesis-style Kaggle writeup analyzing failure spectra (naïve compliance vs. strategic refusal) supported by original flowcharts and system diagrams -Engineered adversarial prompting pipelines to test safety boundaries of GPT-oss-20B Created a writeup on the failure spectra (naïve compliance vs. strategic refusal) tested through verifiable, measurable technical exploits such as Chain of Thought Forgery, access to agent tools (shell scripts), and other technical exploits Show less

### Gemma 3n Multiverse: Democratizing Genius | Google AI Edge Hackathon 2024 | Creator & Lead Architect

Jun 2025 - Aug 2025

-Offline-first AI system bringing proactive intelligence to underserved communities worldwide Engineered cross-device consciousness mesh between MacBook and Google Pixel 9a, creating first-of-its-kind distributed edge AI system with 20+ specialized agent personalities -Architected proactive intelligence engine with 3-round analysis system: context understanding → strategy development → goal synthesis -Deployed native React Native app on Google Pixel 9a with 100% offline capability, supporting 50+ languages for disaster response, rural education, and privacy-critical use cases -Implemented hybrid AI pipeline: Qwen 3 1.7B (context analysis) → Gemma 3n E4B (main reasoning) → specialized agent responses -Impact: Democratizing genius-level AI for Nigeria students, Bangladesh teachers, Kenya climate activists Tech Stack: Google Gemma 3n, React Native + Expo, FastAPI, Ollama, Streamlit, ChromaDB Show less

### Kaggle Jigsaw Subreddit Violation Detection — LLM Fine-Tuning Pipeline

Jun 2025 - Aug 2025

-Fine-tuned Qwen 2.5–0.5B instruct, Qwen 3–0.6B (embeddings) with top K similarity, and DeBERTa V3 using LoRA/PEFT + vLLM. -Utilized GPTQ4 int quantized models with vLLM for memory usage -Experimented with fine tuning Qwen 3 4B with LoRA/PEFT on Lambda A100 GPU -Built weighted-ensemble scoring pipeline achieving 0.91 ROC-AUC and ranking 1028 / 2445 globally. Demonstrated hands-on infrastructure experience with LLM fine-tuning, batching, memory optimization, and GPU throughput tuning.

### Supermind Smart Chat with Claude and OpenAI API

Mar 2025 - Mar 2025

-Zero-cost text-to-speech automation leveraging AWS, Azure, and GCP free tiers -Scaled free TTS capabilities from 10K chars (ElevenLabs) to 12.1 million chars/month across three cloud providers — 1,210x improvement in processing capacity -Reduced 30–60 minute manual processes to <2 minutes via intelligent orchestration -Implemented audio layering with pydub for therapy, motivation, and podcast content -Tech Stack: Python, AWS (Polly, IAM), GCP (TTS), Azure (Cognitive Services), pydub, FFmpeg Show less

## Projects

### TrustVault - Land Regsistry and Document Verification platform using Blockchain and ipfs

Nov 2024 - Mar 2025

TrustVault is a blockchain-based document verification and property registry system that leverages Ethereum smart contracts for secure, transparent, and tamper-proof record-keeping. TrustVault provides a comprehensive solution for: - Property Registration & Verification: Digitally register property details with blockchain-backed verification - Property Ownership Transfer: Securely transfer property ownership with blockchain records - Document Verification: Authenticate and store important documents with cryptographic proof - Government Integration: Dedicated portal for government officials to verify and process requests The system combines traditional database storage with blockchain verification to create a hybrid architecture that benefits from the security of blockchain while maintaining the performance and flexibility of traditional systems. Show less

### OneStop - Student project management platform

Student Project Management Platform Overview This project aims to develop a web-based platform for student project management, specifically designed to tackle the common challenges students face while handling academic projects. It offers an all-in-one solution to plan, track, manage, and collaborate on student projects efficiently. The platform is built to provide a user-friendly dashboard equipped with essential tools that simplify and streamline the project management process, ensuring better productivity and organization. Features Task Scheduler & Management Efficiently organize, assign, and prioritize project tasks to ensure timely completion. Collaborator Management Add and manage team members to collaborate on the same project. Visual Project Tracking Includes dynamic Gantt Charts, Bar Graphs, and Line Graphs for visual representation of project progress. Resource Management Monitor and allocate resources such as time, materials, and manpower effectively. Budget Estimation & Financial Monitoring Tools to estimate project budgets, track cash inflow and outflow, and manage expenses responsibly. Team Chat Room A built-in chat system to facilitate real-time communication between team members. Show less

### Scholar.Ai -Research paper analyses and assistant

ScholarAI Research Assistant is a Chrome extension built to help researchers evaluate the credibility of academic papers faster. It uses Google’s Gemini API to analyze methodology, detect potential bias, and highlight weak claims — all within the browser tab. The goal was to make research review less time-consuming without replacing human judgment. I designed the system around reliability and interpretability — keeping the interface minimal, responsive, and distraction-free. Developed a Chrome extension that leverages Google Gemini to evaluate research paper credibility. Integrated prompt-engineered LLM outputs with a lightweight front-end UI for real-time bias and methodology analysis. Achieved 95% accuracy across 200+ tested publications. Show less

# Sofia Stoica

CS student @ Illinois CS| Research Intern at BLENDER Lab doing multi-modal, NLP, and CV research

Research Assistant at University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign

Oakland, California, United States (US)

154 connections • 157 followers

## About

I went to Proof School (https://www.proofschool.org/academics-3), a math high school, where I did 2 hours a day of advanced to undergraduate-level math, giving me a strong mathematical background. For example, I took Linear Algebra, Theory of Partitions, and Dynamics of Differential Equations. In addition, I am a voracious learner when it comes to computer science, especially machine learning (ML), and research. In high school, I worked on two challenging ML research projects: one where I implemented and fine-tuned machine learning algorithms for stock price prediction of a tech company, which was published, and another, which focused on protection against adversarial attacks in Computer Vision. Now, I am researching how to bridge the gap between perception and reasoning in Large Multimodal Language Models (LMLs) in Professor Heng Ji's BLENDER LAB (https://blender.cs.illinois.edu/). The goal is to build large language models that meet the demand of superior reasoning over text and image/video domain to help people become more effective in their work and open opportunities in all fields. My first project, PAPO (https://mikewangwzhl.github.io/PAPO/) code and project page has been released! Stay tuned for the conference update and news about my second work! Spurred by my passion to learn more, during my high school career, I have shadowed three undergraduate ML classes, one during an ML class and the other two during my senior year independent study; and I am currently shadowing two (Spring 2025). During my freshman year of college, I read and attended some classes from the Systems for Gen AI, Advanced topics in NLP. During the summer, I learned RL through Barto & Sutton's Sutton's book and university of Alberta Coursera course. The past summer (2024) I even completed over 200 LeetCode problems. I don't learn to just learn. I learn for my passion and desire to make an impact. Let us push forward together to create something wonderful, meaningful, and helpful.

Total Experience: 1 year and 5 months

## Publications

### Perception-Aware Policy Optimization for Multimodal Reasoning

July 8, 2025

Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has proven to be a highly effective strategy for endowing Large Language Models (LLMs) with robust multi-step reasoning abilities. However, its design and optimizations remain tailored to purely textual domains, resulting in suboptimal performance when applied to multimodal reasoning tasks. In particular, we observe that a major source of error in current multimodal reasoning lies in the perception of visual inputs. To address this bottleneck, we propose Perception-Aware Policy Optimization (PAPO), a simple yet effective extension of GRPO that encourages the model to learn to perceive while learning to reason, entirely from internal supervision signals. Notably, PAPO does not rely on additional data curation, external reward models, or proprietary models. Specifically, we introduce the Implicit Perception Loss in the form of a KL divergence term to the GRPO objective, which, despite its simplicity, yields significant overall improvements (4.4%) on diverse multimodal benchmarks. The improvements are more pronounced, approaching 8.0%, on tasks with high vision dependency. We also observe a substantial reduction (30.5%) in perception errors, indicating improved perceptual capabilities with PAPO. We conduct comprehensive analysis of PAPO and identify a unique loss hacking issue, which we rigorously analyze and mitigate through a Double Entropy Loss. Overall, our work introduces a deeper integration of perception-aware supervision into RLVR learning objectives and lays the groundwork for a new RL framework that encourages visually grounded reasoning. Project page: https://mikewangwzhl.github.io/PAPO/ Show less

View Publication

### Using Historical Data for Stock Prediction of a Tech Company

January 10, 2024

In this paper, we use historical data to predict the stock price of a tech company. To this end, we use a dataset consisting of the stock prices over the past five years of 10 major tech companies: Adobe, Amazon, Apple, Facebook, Google, Microsoft, Netflix, Oracle, Salesforce, and Tesla. We implemented and tested three models – a linear regressor model, a k-nearest neighbor model (KNN), and a sequential neural network – and two algorithms – Multiplicative Weight Update and AdaBoost. We found that the sequential neural network performed the best, with a testing error of 0.18%. Interestingly, the linear model performed the second best with a testing error of 0.73%. These results show that using historical data is enough to obtain high accuracies, and a simple algorithm like linear regression has a performance similar to more sophisticated models while taking less time and resources to implement. The paper was also accepted to the International Conference in Computer Science, Machine Learning, and Algorithms (https://waset.org/computer-science-machine-learning-and-algorithms-conference-in-february-2023-in-washington) Show less

View Publication

## Projects

### Private IoT Cloud

Mar 2016 - Present

Designed, developed and deployed an IoT solution hosted by a private cloud. This solution is about connecting multiple industrial plants to the Cloud to fetch sensor & metering data. The system can control the industrial plants in addition to collecting data. I have worked as system architect, designer and developed some core modules.

### API Manager — Scalable Web API Manager

Nov 2015 - Present

Designed, developed & deployed a web API manager which fronts the cloud APIs and enforces authentication, authorization, quota and throttling. It is scalable, robust and efficient. I have designed and implemented a unique feature of validating access key without querying the db. Implements OAUTH 2.0

### Task Scheduler — REST API Task Scheduler

Feb 2014 - Present

Designed, developed, tested & deployed a task scheduler which reads its tasks from XML task files and schedules it on the specified time/frequency. It supports nested operation and can call RESTful API’s as operations.

### Hedwig — Jabber Packet transformation & Relay Server

Sep 2013 - Present

Designed, developed, tested & deployed a transformation and relay server to be used as cloud-to-device messaging channel. Hedwig remains connected to Ejabberd server and relays messages to IoT devices. It can transform message to multiple formats including XML, JSON. Hedwig can also be hooked to a RabbitMQ Queue on the cloud side.

### Telehealth — A PoC to showcase IoT Health Assistant

Sep 2011 - Present

Designed, developed & presented a proof-of-concept application to showcase how IoT can work in health monitoring devices. The application was an Android App which could fetch readings from Blood Pressure monitor, Pulse-Oximeter & Weighing scale and post to the cloud. It could show graphs about historical data and suggest medications advised by the doctor.

### Test Bench — Energy Meter Testing JIG Control Bench

Dec 2009 - Present

Designed, developed, tested & deployed a control panel for operating an Energy Meter Testing JIG. Energy meters are hooked with the JIG and the control panel executes a selectable set of tests. The control panel reads the result and saves it in DB. Test passing criteria is defined in metadata. Reports can also be generated.

I belong to Project department as System Engineer. My main tasks as SE are: - Support the pre-sales team for the technical contract - Support the sales team for technical presentations of our solution to customers - Customer communication for all technical aspects during the life time of a project - Follow-up with RD team when a special PCB/Software is required for a project (from requirements definition to validation of the board/SW) - Create System definition schematics (overall schematics of the loadbox) - Create Loadbox definition file (which includes the detailed schematics for the production) - Create harness definition file - Follow-up with production team during the loadbox/harness production - Create loadbox validation plan (to make sure the loadbox production is in line with the design) - Perform the loadbox validation plan - Create the test case into the Art Logics software (ALTS) - Perform the full validation of the bench (loadbox + DUT* + ALTS) - Commissioning of the bench with the customer - After sale service when questions/issues from customers *Device Under Test Show less

# Luca Marzari

PhD candidate @ University of Verona

## About

My main research interests focus on developing efficient and reliable methods for verifying and enhancing the explainability of deep neural networks (DNNs). I also developed probabilistic verification algorithms with strong theoretical guarantees to bridge the gap between Formal Verification of DNNs and safe deep reinforcement learning. The results of my research resulted in several publications at top-level international conferences and prestigious collaborations with world-leading universities. Programming languages: Python (PyTorch, TensorFlow), Java, Flutter / Dart, C, PostgreSQL, C ++, LaTeX and Visual Basic. Cost functions are driving the successes of recent Safe Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) literature. However, the non-trivial design of a cost function led to using sparse and thus less informative cost signals, which usually result in poor performances of the learning agent. The research aims to find a novel metric to quantify the number of incorrect actions an agent chooses over desired safety properties. ## Publications

### The full list of publications is available here:

August 26, 2023

View Publication

### The #DNN-Verification Problem: Counting Unsafe Inputs for Deep Neural Networks

April 19, 2023

Deep Neural Networks are increasingly adopted in critical tasks that require a high level of safety, e.g., autonomous driving. While state-of-the-art verifiers can be employed to check whether a DNN is unsafe w.r.t. some given property (i.e., whether there is at least one unsafe input configuration), their yes/no output is not informative enough for other purposes, such as shielding, model selection, or training improvements. In this paper, we introduce the #DNN-Verification problem, which involves counting the number of input configurations of a DNN that result in a violation of a particular safety property. We analyze the complexity of this problem and propose a novel approach that returns the exact count of violations. Due to the #P- completeness of the problem, we also propose a randomized, approximate method that provides a provable probabilistic bound of the correct count while significantly reducing computational requirements. We present experimental results on a set of safety-critical benchmarks that demonstrate the effectiveness of our approximate method and evaluate the tightness of the bound. Show less

View Publication

### Curriculum Learning for Safe Mapless Navigation

This work investigates the effects of Curriculum Learning (CL)-based approaches on the agent's performance. In particular, we focus on the safety aspect of robotic mapless navigation, comparing over a standard end-to-end (E2E) training strategy. To this end, we present a CL approach that leverages Transfer of Learning (ToL) and fine-tuning in a Unity-based simulation with the Robotnik Kairos as a robotic agent. For a fair comparison, our evaluation considers an equal computational demand for every learning approach (i.e., the same number of interactions and difficulty of the environments) and confirms that our CL-based method that uses ToL outperforms the E2E methodology. In particular, we improve the average success rate and the safety of the trained policy, resulting in 10% fewer collisions in unseen testing scenarios. To further confirm these results, we employ a formal verification tool to quantify the number of correct behaviors of Reinforcement Learning policies over desired specifications. Show less

View Publication

### Towards Hierarchical Task Decomposition using Deep Reinforcement Learning for Pick and Place Subtasks

Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is emerging as a promising approach to generate adaptive behaviors for robotic platforms. However, a major drawback of using DRL is the data-hungry training regime that requires millions of trial and error attempts, which is impractical when running experiments on robotic systems. Learning from Demonstrations (LfD) has been introduced to solve this issue by cloning the behavior of expert demonstrations. However, LfD requires a large number of demonstrations that are difficult to be acquired since dedicated complex setups are required. To overcome these limitations, we propose a multi-subtask reinforcement learning methodology where complex pick and place tasks can be decomposed into low-level subtasks. These subtasks are parametrized as expert networks and learned via DRL methods. Trained subtasks are then combined by a high-level choreographer to accomplish the intended pick and place task considering different initial configurations. As a testbed, we use a pick and place robotic simulator to demonstrate our methodology and show that our method outperforms a benchmark methodology based on LfD in terms of sample-efficiency. We transfer the learned policy to the real robotic system and demonstrate robust grasping using various geometric-shaped objects. Show less

View Publication

Project Link

## Publications

### WAE-PCN: Wasserstein-autoencoded Pareto Conditioned Networks

Published by: Mathieu Reymond

May 30, 2023

In real-world problems, decision makers often have to balance multiple objectives, which can result in trade-offs. One approach to finding a compromise is to use a multi-objective approach, which builds a set of all optimal trade-offs called a Pareto front. Learning the Pareto front requires exploring many different parts of the state- space, which can be time-consuming and increase the chances of encountering undesired or dangerous parts of the state-space. In this preliminary work, we propose a method that combines two frameworks, Pareto Conditioned Networks (PCN) and Wasserstein auto-encoded MDPs (WAE-MDPs), to efficiently learn all possible trade-offs while providing formal guarantees on the learned poli- cies. The proposed method learns the Pareto-optimal policies while providing safety and performance guarantees, especially towards unexpected events, in the multi-objective setting. Show less

View Publication

### Wasserstein Auto-encoded MDPs: Formal Verification of Efficiently Distilled RL Policies with Many-sided Guarantees.

Published by: Ann Nowé, Guillermo A. Pérez

February 1, 2023

Although deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has many success stories, the large-scale deployment of policies learned through these advanced techniques in safety-critical scenarios is hindered by their lack of formal guarantees. Variational Markov Decision Processes (VAE-MDPs) are discrete latent space models that provide a reliable framework for distilling formally verifiable controllers from any RL policy. While the related guarantees address relevant practical aspects such as the satisfaction of performance and safety properties, the VAE approach suffers from several learning flaws (posterior collapse, slow learning speed, poor dynamics estimates), primarily due to the absence of abstraction and representation guarantees to support latent optimization. We introduce the Wasserstein auto-encoded MDP (WAE-MDP), a latent space model that fixes those issues by minimizing a penalized form of the optimal transport between the behaviors of the agent executing the original policy and the distilled policy, for which the formal guarantees apply. Our approach yields bisimulation guarantees while learning the distilled policy, allowing concrete optimization of the abstraction and representation model quality. Our experiments show that, besides distilling policies up to 10 times faster, the latent model quality is indeed better in general. Moreover, we present experiments from a simple time-to-failure verification algorithm on the latent space. The fact that our approach enables such simple verification techniques highlights its applicability. Show less

View Publication

### Distillation of RL Policies with Formal Guarantees (Extended Abstract)

Published by: Ann Nowé, Guillermo A. Pérez

November 7, 2022

While reinforcement learning (RL) has been applied to a wide range of challenging domains, from game playing to real-world applications such as effective canal control, more widespread deployment in the real world is hampered by the lack of guarantees provided with the learned policies. Although there are RL algorithms which have limit-convergence guarantees in the discrete setting (and even in some continuous settings with function approximation), these are lost when applying more advanced techniques which make use of general nonlinear function approximators to deal with continuous Markov decision processes (MDPs) such as deep-RL. In this work, we apply such advanced RL algorithms to unknown continuous MDPs with (safety constrained) reachability or discounted-reward objectives, and we consider the challenge of simplifying and verifying RL policies. Our goal is to enable model checking by learning an accurate, tractable model of the environment. Show less

View Publication

### Distillation of RL Policies with Formal Guarantees via Variational Abstraction of Markov Decision Processes

Published by: Ann Nowé, Guillermo A. Pérez

June 28, 2022

We consider the challenge of policy simplification and verification in the context of policies learned through reinforcement learning (RL) in continuous environments. In well-behaved settings, RL algorithms have convergence guarantees in the limit. While these guarantees are valuable, they are insufficient for safety-critical applications. Furthermore, they are lost when applying advanced techniques such as deep-RL. To recover guarantees when applying advanced RL algorithms to more complex environments with (i) reachability, (ii) safety-constrained reachability, or (iii) discounted-reward objectives, we build upon the DeepMDP framework to derive new bisimulation bounds between the unknown environment and a learned discrete latent model of it. Our bisimulation bounds enable the application of formal methods for Markov decision processes. Finally, we show how one can use a policy obtained via state-of-the-art RL to efficiently train a variational autoencoder that yields a discrete latent model with provably approximately correct bisimulation guarantees. Additionally, we obtain a distilled version of the policy for the latent model. Show less

View Publication

### A Framework for Flexibly Guiding Learning Agents

Published by: Mahmoud Elbarbari, Ivo Vervlimmeren, Kyriakos Efthymiadis, Bram Vanderborght, Ann Nowé

June 7, 2022

Reinforcement Learning (RL) enables artificial agents to learn through direct interaction with the environment. However, it usually does not scale up well to large problems due to its sampling inefficiency. Reward Shaping is a well-established approach that allows for more efficient learning by incorporating domain knowledge in RL agents via supplementary rewards. In this work, we propose a novel methodology that automatically generates reward shaping functions from user-provided Linear Temporal Logic on finite traces (LTLf formulas). LTLf in our work serves as a rich language that allows the user to communicate domain knowledge to the learning agent. In both single and multi-agent settings, we demonstrate that our approach performs at least as well as the baseline approach while providing essential advantages in terms of flexibility and ease of use. We elaborate on some of these advantages empirically by demonstrating that our approach can handle domain knowledge with different levels of accuracy, and provides the user with the flexibility to express aspects of uncertainty in the provided advice. Show less

View Publication

### Life is Random, Time is Not: Markov Decision Processes with Window Objectives

Published by: Thomas Brihaye, Youssouf Oualhadj, Mickael Randour 

December 14, 2020

The window mechanism was introduced by Chatterjee et al. to strengthen classical game objectives with time bounds. It permits to synthesize system controllers that exhibit acceptable behaviors within a configurable time frame, all along their infinite execution, in contrast to the traditional objectives that only require correctness of behaviors in the limit. The window concept has proved its interest in a variety of two-player zero-sum games because it enables reasoning about such time bounds in system specifications, but also thanks to the increased tractability that it usually yields. In this work, we extend the window framework to stochastic environments by considering Markov decision processes. A fundamental problem in this context is the threshold probability problem: given an objective it aims to synthesize strategies that guarantee satisfying runs with a given probability. We solve it for the usual variants of window objectives, where either the time frame is set as a parameter, or we ask if such a time frame exists. We develop a generic approach for window-based objectives and instantiate it for the classical mean-payoff and parity objectives, already considered in games. Our work paves the way to a wide use of the window mechanism in stochastic models. Show less

View Publication

### Simple Strategies in Multi-Objective MDPs

Published by: Joost-Pieter Katoen, Tim Quatmann, Mickael Randour

April 30, 2020

We consider the verification of multiple expected reward objectives at once on Markov decision processes (MDPs). This enables a trade-off analysis among multiple objectives by obtaining the Pareto front. We focus on strategies that are easy to employ and implement. That is, strategies that are pure (no randomization) and have bounded memory. We show that checking whether a point is achievable by a pure stationary strategy is NP-complete, even for two objectives, and we provide an MILP encoding to solve the corresponding problem. The bounded memory case can be reduced to the stationary one by a product construction. Experimental results using Storm and Gurobi show the feasibility of our algorithms. Show less

View Publication

### Life is Random, Time is Not: Markov Decision Processes with Window Objectives

Published by: Thomas Brihaye, Youssouf Oualhadj, Mickael Randour

August 30, 2019

The window mechanism was introduced by Chatterjee et al. [1] to strengthen classical game objectives with time bounds. It permits to synthesize system controllers that exhibit acceptable behaviors within a configurable time frame, all along their infinite execution, in contrast to the traditional objectives that only require correctness of behaviors in the limit. The window concept has proved its interest in a variety of two-player zero-sum games, thanks to the ability to reason about such time bounds in system specifications, but also the increased tractability that it usually yields. In this work, we extend the window framework to stochastic environments by considering the fundamental threshold probability problem in Markov decision processes for window objectives. That is, given such an objective, we want to synthesize strategies that guarantee satisfying runs with a given probability. We solve this problem for the usual variants of window objectives, where either the time frame is set as a parameter, or we ask if such a time frame exists. We develop a generic approach for window-based objectives and instantiate it for the classical mean-payoff and parity objectives, already considered in games. Our work paves the way to a wide use of the window mechanism in stochastic models. [1] Krishnendu Chatterjee, Laurent Doyen, Mickael Randour, and Jean-François Raskin. Looking at mean-payoff and total-payoff through windows. Inf. Comput., 242:25-52, 2015. Show less
