# Charlie Hepburn

Researcher in Safe Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning | PhD in Offline Reinforcement Learning, University of Warwick

Postdoctoral Research Associate at University of Warwick

United Kingdom (GB)

120 connections • 122 followers

## About

Researcher in safe multi-agent reinforcement learning at the Department of Statistics, University of Warwick. Completed a PhD in offline reinforcement learning at the Mathematics Institute, University of Warwick

Total Experience: 1 year and 7 months

## Experience

### Postdoctoral Research Associate at University of Warwick (Current)

Jun 2024 - Present • 1 year and 6 months

Company: 5001-10,000 employees • Founded 1965 • Educational • Higher Education

I developed an RL agent to manage air traffic in European airspace. The agent must handle a large, dynamic number of aircraft and ensure collision avoidance. As the simulator is built from scratch, state representations and reward functions must be carefully hand-designed. I designed a multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithm (based on MAPPO) to control the flights in the airspace. States were represented as a graph where each agent is a node in the graph - this formulation helps with scalability and a changing number of agents throughout training. Policies were modelled using a GNN. Show less

Department: Research • Level: Specialist

## Education

### Doctor of Philosophy - PhD, Deep Reinforcement Learning at University of Warwick

2021 - 2025 • 4 years

coventry, GB

Thesis title: State-constrained Offline Deep Reinforcement Learning Developed a novel framework for offline reinforcement learning that constrains based on the state distribution rather than the state-action distribution, mitigating distributional shift and yielding closer-to-optimal policies. Introduced two branches of the state-constrained method: one based on data augmentation (MBTS) and the other on Q-learning (StaCQ). Implemented/benchmarked algorithms in Python using PyTorch, NumPy, OpenAI Gym, Weights & Biases, Git. Awarded Best Talk at the Centre for Doctoral Training (CDT) annual conference in both first and third year. First-authored three peer-reviewed papers on offline reinforcement learning Show less

2020 - 2021 • 1 year

coventry, GB

Dissertation: A Critical Analysis of Selected Offline Reinforcement Learning Algorithms. Supervised by: Prof. Giovanni Montana.

### Master of Mathematics - MMath, Mathematics, First class at The University of Edinburgh

2016 - 2020 • 4 years

Edinburgh, GB

Dissertation: Randomized Iterative Methods for Linear Systems. Supervised by Dr. Aretha Teckentrup.

# Jaeyoung Lee

Research Fellow at the Centre for AI Fundamentals at UManchester, UK (2024.01~)

Research Fellow at The University of Manchester

United Kingdom (GB)

106 connections • 108 followers

## About

A research topic that goes through my entire career is reinforcement learning (RL). I'm currently working as a Research Fellow at the Centre for AI Fundamentals at the University of Manchester (2024-present), mainly studying interdisciplinary areas including AI-assistant and RL. Before then, as a Research Associate/Postdoctoral Fellow, I focused on safe RL and its applications to autonomous driving at the University of Waterloo (2018.01~2022.03). Previous studies include reinforcement learning from AI- and control-perspectives at the University of Alberta as a Postdoctoral Fellow (2015.08~2017.12) and Yonsei University as a graduate student (2007.09~2015.08). My current research interests include, but not limited to, AI-assistant, (deep / constrained) reinforcement and imitation learning for safety-critical systems; explainable RL; game-theoretic decision-making; RL and optimal control; multi-agent systems.

Total Experience: 8 years and 5 months

## Publications

### Safe Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Quantitatively Verified Constraints

May 3, 2023

PhD thesis detailing three original approaches to safe multi-agent reinforcement learning.

View Publication

### Assured Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning with Robust Agent-Interaction Adaptability

July 27, 2022

Multi-agent reinforcement learning facilitates agents learning to solve complex decision-making problems requiring collaboration. However, reinforcement learning methods are underpinned by stochastic mechanisms, making them unsuitable for safety-critical domains. To solve this issue, approaches such as assured multi-agent reinforcement learning, which utilises quantitative verification to produce formal guarantees of safety requirements during the agents learning process, have been developed. However, this approach relies on accurate knowledge about the environment to be effectively used which can be detrimental if this knowledge is inaccurate. Therefore, we developed an extension to assured multi-agent reinforcement learning called agent interaction driven adaptability, an automated process to securing reliable safety constraints, allowing inaccurate and missing knowledge to be used without detriment. Our preliminary results showcase the ability of agent interaction driven adaptability to allow safe multi-agent reinforcement learning to be utilised in safety-critical scenarios. Show less

View Publication

### Assured Deep Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Safe Robotic Systems

June 7, 2022

Using multi-agent reinforcement learning to find solutions to complex decision-making problems in shared environments has become standard practice in many scenarios. However, this is not the case in safety-critical scenarios, where the reinforcement learning process, which uses stochastic mechanisms, could lead to highly unsafe outcomes. We proposed a novel, safe multi-agent reinforcement learning approach named Assured Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (AMARL) to address this issue. Distinct from other safe multi-agent reinforcement learning approaches, AMARL utilises quantitative verification, a model checking technique that guarantees agent compliance of safety, performance, and non-functional requirements, both during and after the learning process. We have previously evaluated AMARL in patrolling domains with various multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithms for both homogeneous and heterogeneous systems. In this work we extend AMARL through the use of deep multi-agent reinforcement learning. This approach is particularly appropriate for systems in which the rewards are sparse and hence extends the applicability of AMARL. We evaluate our approach within a new search and collection domain which demonstrates promising results in safety standards and performance compared to algorithms not using AMARL. Show less

View Publication

### Utilising Assured Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning within Safety-Critical Scenarios

September 8, 2021

Multi-agent reinforcement learning allows a team of agents to learn how to work together to solve complex decision-making problems in a shared environment. However, this learning process utilises stochastic mechanisms, meaning that its use in safety-critical domains can be problematic. To overcome this issue, we propose an Assured Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (AMARL) approach that uses a model checking technique called quantitative verification to provide formal guarantees of agent compliance with safety, performance, and other non-functional requirements during and after the reinforcement learning process. We demonstrate the applicability of our AMARL approach in three different patrolling navigation domains in which multi-agent systems must learn to visit key areas by using different types of reinforcement learning algorithms (temporal difference learning, game theory, and direct policy search). Furthermore, we compare the effectiveness of these algorithms when used in combination with and without our approach. Our extensive experiments with both homogeneous and heterogeneous multi-agent systems of different sizes show that the use of AMARL leads to safety requirements being consistently satisfied and to better overall results than standard reinforcement learning. Show less

View Publication

### Assured Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning using Quantitative Verification.

Feb 2021

In multi-agent reinforcement learning, several agents converge together towards optimal policies that solve complex decision-making problems. This convergence process is inherently stochastic, meaning that its use in safety-critical domains can be problematic. To address this issue, we introduce a new approach that combines multi-agent reinforcement learning with a formal verification technique termed quantitative verification. Our assured multi-agent reinforcement learning approach constrains agent behaviours in ways that ensure the satisfaction of requirements associated with the safety, reliability, and other non-functional aspects of the decision-making problem being solved. The approach comprises three stages. First, it models the problem as an abstract Markov decision process, allowing quantitative verification to be applied. Next, this abstract model is used to synthesise a policy which satisfies safety, reliability, and performance constraints. Finally, the synthesised policy is used to constrain agent behaviour within the low-level problem with a greatly lowered risk of constraint violations. Show less

View Publication

### Reinforcement Learning with Quantitative Verification for Assured Multi-Agent Policies

Feb 2021

In multi-agent reinforcement learning, several agents converge together towards optimal policies that solve complex decision-making problems. This convergence process is inherently stochastic, meaning that its use in safety-critical domains can be problematic. To address this issue, we introduce a new approach that combines multi-agent reinforcement learning with a formal verification technique termed quantitative verification. Our assured multi-agent reinforcement learning approach constrains agent behaviours in ways that ensure the satisfaction of requirements associated with the safety, reliability, and other non-functional aspects of the decision-making problem being solved. The approach comprises three stages. First, it models the problem as an abstract Markov decision process, allowing quantitative verification to be applied. Next, this abstract model is used to synthesise a policy which satisfies safety, reliability, and performance constraints. Finally, the synthesised policy is used to constrain agent behaviour within the low-level problem with a greatly lowered risk of constraint violations. We demonstrate our approach using a safety-critical multi-agent patrolling problem. Show less

View Publication

Bobigny, Île-de-France, France

Tech Leader of a team of 5 persons, the DRIL team (Decision with Reinforcement and Imitation learning). Nominated Valeo expert in Reinforcement Learning (Jan 2023) due to exceptional achievements, technical skills and internal knowledge transmission. Developed and integrated on a real car, a Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithm for autonomous parking. Demonstrated a DRL agent driving on a real car in urban environment (10km without human intervention).

Department: Research • Level: Senior

### PHD Candidate at Valeo

Jan 2018 - Apr 2021 • 3 years and 3 months

Bobigny (Valeo), Paris Luxembourg (Mines ParisTech)

Company: 10,001+ employees • Founded 1923 • Public Company • Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing

PhD Student on "End-to-End driving with Deep Reinforcement Learning". The aim of this thesis is to teach to deep neural network to control a car (lateral and longitudinal) by feeding him only with the raw data from the sensors (camera, LIDAR, radar...). One really promising way of doing so is to use Deep Reinforcement Learning algorithm on a simulator and then transfer the learning from the simulation to a car in the real world. (see the paper "End-to-End Race Driving with Deep Reinforcement Learning" accepted at ICRA 2018) I developed a new distributed algorithm coined "Rainbow-IQN Ape-X" which reach state-of-the-art performance on the Atari benchmark and published an article entitled Is "Deep Reinforcement Learning Really Superhuman on Atari?" at the "Deep Reinforcement Learning" workshop at NeurIPS 2019. I used this DRL algorithm to participate to the CARLA Challenge 2019 where I finally finished first on the "Camera-Only" track and second on the "Camera Lidar" track. This work led to another article "End-to-End Model-Free Reinforcement Learning for Urban Driving using Implicit Affordances" published at the conference CVPR 2020. Show less

## Publications

### End-to-End Model-Free Reinforcement Learning for Urban Driving using Implicit Affordances

Published by: Emilie W., Fabien Moutarde

March 16, 2020

Reinforcement Learning (RL) aims at learning an optimal behavior policy from its own experiments and not rule-based control methods. However, there is no RL algorithm yet capable of handling a task as difficult as urban driving. We present a novel technique, coined implicit affordances, to effectively leverage RL for urban driving thus including lane keeping, pedestrians and vehicles avoidance, and traffic light detection. To our knowledge we are the first to present a successful RL agent handling such a complex task especially regarding the traffic light detection. Furthermore, we have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method by winning the Camera Only track of the CARLA challenge. Show less

View Publication

### Is Deep Reinforcement Learning Really Superhuman on Atari?

Published by: Emilie W., Fabien Moutarde

November 8, 2019

Consistent and reproducible evaluation of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is not straightforward. In the Arcade Learning Environment (ALE), small changes in environment parameters such as stochasticity or the maximum allowed play time can lead to very different performance. In this work, we discuss the difficulties of comparing different agents trained on ALE. In order to take a step further towards reproducible and comparable DRL, we introduce SABER, a Standardized Atari BEnchmark for general Reinforcement learning algorithms. Our methodology extends previous recommendations and contains a complete set of environment parameters as well as train and test procedures. We then use SABER to evaluate the current state of the art, Rainbow. Furthermore, we introduce a human world records baseline, and argue that previous claims of expert or superhuman performance of DRL might not be accurate. Finally, we propose Rainbow-IQN by extending Rainbow with Implicit Quantile Networks (IQN) leading to new state-of-the-art performance. Source code is available for reproducibility. Show less

View Publication

### End to End Vehicle Lateral Control Using a Single Fisheye Camera

November 1, 2018

Convolutional neural networks are commonly used to control the steering angle for autonomous cars. Most of the time, multiple long range cameras are used to generate lateral failure cases. In this paper we present a novel model to generate this data and label augmentation using only one short range fisheye camera. We present our simulator and how it can be used as a consistent metric for lateral end-to-end control evaluation. Experiments are conducted on a custom dataset corresponding to more than 10000 km and 200 hours of open road driving. Finally we evaluate this model on real world driving scenarios, open road and a custom test track with challenging obstacle avoidance and sharp turns. In our simulator based on real-world videos, the final model was capable of more than 99% autonomy on urban road. Show less

View Publication

### End-to-End Race Driving with Deep Reinforcement Learning

August 31, 2018

We present research using the latest reinforcement learning algorithm for end-to-end driving without any mediated perception (object recognition, scene understanding). The newly proposed reward and learning strategies lead together to faster convergence and more robust driving using only RGB image from a forward facing camera. An Asynchronous Actor Critic (A3C) framework is used to learn the car control in a physically and graphically realistic rally game, with the agents evolving simultaneously on tracks with a variety of road structures (turns, hills), graphics (seasons, location) and physics (road adherence). A thorough evaluation is conducted and generalization is proven on unseen tracks and using legal speed limits. Open loop tests on real sequences of images show some domain adaption capability of our method. Show less

View Publication

### End-To-End Driving in a Realistic Racing Game With Deep Reinforcement Learning

July 21, 2017

For autonomous driving the classical paradigm is to use a chain of perception, planning and control; but recent deep learning progress lets foresee an end-to-end alternative to map sensor inputs directly to low-level control of robots. End-to-end driving was showcased in the car racing game TORCS using Reinforcement Learning but its physics and graphics lack realism. We propose a method benefiting from latest asynchronous learning to train an end-to-end agent in the context of a realistic car racing game - World Rally Championship 6 (WRC6). Show less

View Publication

## Honors & Awards

Jul 2019

- First place in the "Camera Only" track - Second place in the "Camera + Lidar" track

results.title: Auguste Lehuger | Team Lead @ Valeo RL Lab
results.author: Auguste Lehuger
results.text:
# Auguste Lehuger

Team Lead @ Valeo RL Lab

Team Leader RL Lab at Valeo

Paris, Île-de-France, France (FR)

500 connections • 868 followers

## About

Exploring the potential benefits of Reinforcement Learning for industrial innovation.

Total Experience: 6 years and 6 months

## Experience

### Team Leader RL Lab at Valeo (Current)

Aug 2024 - Present • 1 year and 5 months

Paris, Île-de-France, France

Company: 10,001+ employees • Founded 1923 • Public Company • Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing

+ 💾 Leading the development of PCB Panelizer, AI algorithm to automate circuit board industrial design. Deployed worldwide, saving up to 20k tons of CO2eq per year.

### Technical Lead at Valeo

Jul 2023 - Jul 2024 • 1 year

Company: 10,001+ employees • Founded 1923 • Public Company • Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing

+🎓 Started supervising PhD on RL Planner for Autonomous Driving.

Department: Engineering and Technical • Level: Senior

### Master Thesis Project at Imperial College London

Mar 2020 - Oct 2020 • 7 months

London, England, United Kingdom

Company: 5001-10,000 employees • Founded 1907 • Educational • Research Services

Investigate State-of-the-art DRL algorithms and Curiosity-based intrinsic motivation to be evaluated on the Animal-AI testbed. Under the supervision of Matthew Crosby and Benjamin Beyret. http://animalaiolympics.com/AAI During my master thesis, I had the opportunity to research how to improve state encoding in curiosity computation. We concluded that a pre-trained ß-VAE encoding had a critical impact for exploring complex 3D environments, such as the AnimalAI Testbed, compared to the current state-of-the-art curiosity module. An undergoing paper is demonstrating how the variational encoder factorised latent space leads to a 30% sample efficiency gain for maze navigation tasks. -> https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.08568 Show less

Department: Education • Level: Specialist

### RL Research Engineer at Valeo

Jan 2021 - Jul 2023 • 2 years and 6 months

Company: 10,001+ employees • Founded 1923 • Public Company • Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing

🚗 Research on Imitation and Reinforcement Learning algorithms. Building the first RL-trained planner in production for the automotive industry.

Department: Research • Level: Specialist

### Computer Vision Research Engineer at EOS imaging

Apr 2019 - Aug 2019 • 4 months

Région de Montréal, Canada

Company: 51-200 employees • Public Company • Medical Equipment Manufacturing

- Researched sota Computer Vision Algorithm (GAN, ResNets, Mask RCNN) - Proposed an innovative methodology to automatize the Spine 3D Reconstruction - Developed (Python, C++) this method showing 25% precision improvement for lumbar vertebrae EOS is a medical device company that designs, develops, and markets its own imaging system based on Charpak's low radiation protocol. From the radiographs obtained, EOS' experts are able to reconstruct patients' skeleton in 3D to diagnose pathologies and help surgeries. Show less

Department: Engineering and Technical • Level: Specialist

### Full Stack Development Intern at Ippon Technologies USA

Jun 2018 - Aug 2018 • 2 months

Richmond, Virginia, États-Unis

Company: 51-200 employees • Founded 2014 • Privately Held • IT Services and IT Consulting

- Developed an insurance company website with Angular and Spring, using JHipster stack. - Interconnected a chatbot supported by Amazon Alexa. - Pitched this proof-of-concept to a well-known insurance company. Stack: SQL, Angular(JS), Spring (Java) + AWS skills

Department: Engineering and Technical • Level: Intern

### Math Trainer at Passerelles numériques Cambodia

Oct 2016 - Apr 2017 • 6 months

Phnom Penh, Cambodia

Company: 51-200 employees • Founded 2005 • Educational • Higher Education

In 2016, PNC was remodelling their training. As the math trainer, - I was assigned to create a new 90 hours math curriculum. - I had the opportunity to teach it to the 2018 PNC batch (50 students) for 4 months. - I was then assigned to update the written exams and the information sessions we give to the students in the countryside. PNC is a French NGO based in Cambodia which provides education, technical and professional training in the digital sector to young underprivileged people. Show less

Department: Education • Level: Specialist

## Education

### Cycle Pluridisciplinaire d'Etudes Supérieures - Licence de Mathématiques, Mathématiques appliquées, Licence Mention Très Bien at Paris Sciences & Lettres - PSL

2014 - 2016 • 2 years

Paris, FR

### Master of Science - MSc, Machine Learning at Imperial College London

2019 - 2020 • 1 year

London, GB

Relevant Coursework: Machine learning, Deep learning, Reinforcement learning, Computer vision, Natural language processing, Distributed Ledgers, Privacy engineering.

### Cycle Ingénieur Polytechnicien, Mathématiques et Informatique at École Polytechnique

2016 - 2020 • 4 years

FR

Majors: * Mathematics: Stochastic processes, Optimisation, Partial Differential equations, Differential Calculus & Complex Analysis * Computer Science: Algorithms, Advanced programming, Big-data * Machine learning: Statistics, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, Reinforcement Learning Minors: * Humanities: Debating Class, Architecture, Philosophy in Cinema, Engraving * Economy: Macroeconomy, Business economy

Activities: Activities and Societies: Founder of Polytechnique Botanical Garden, Oenology and Cinema Club

## Skills

## Languages

English - Full professional proficiency

Spanish - Limited working proficiency

French - Native or bilingual proficiency
